entries.name: accessibility
entries.description: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance — semantic HTML, ARIA, keyboard navigation, color contrast, screen reader testing

entries.name: action-safety
entries.description: Rules for handling destructive, irreversible, or high-impact actions

entries.name: agentflow-authoring
entries.description: Complete reference for authoring AgentFlow workspaces — file formats, taxonomy, context layers, reference syntax, validation rules

entries.name: api-design
entries.description: Best practices for designing clean, consistent, and evolvable APIs

entries.name: code-search
entries.description: Efficient codebase exploration strategies for finding definitions, usages, and data flows

entries.name: coding-standards
entries.description: Project-wide coding conventions loaded into every agent session

entries.name: context-management
entries.description: How to manage context budget, keep prompts tight, and use progressive disclosure

entries.name: debugging
entries.description: Systematic approach to diagnosing and fixing bugs

entries.name: dependency-management
entries.description: Keep dependencies minimal, audit regularly, pin versions, check licenses, prefer well-maintained packages

entries.name: error-handling
entries.description: Never swallow errors — explicit error handling patterns, guard clauses, custom types, and logging strategy

entries.name: implementation-discipline
entries.description: Best practices for writing production-quality code during implementation

entries.name: prompt-engineering
entries.description: Techniques for writing effective LLM prompts and agent instructions

entries.name: refactoring
entries.description: Safe, incremental code restructuring without changing behavior

entries.name: requirements-elicitation
entries.description: Systematic approach to gathering, structuring, and validating software requirements

entries.name: security-review
entries.description: Systematic security audit checklist for code and infrastructure

entries.name: task-decomposition
entries.description: Break down a technical design into ordered, atomic implementation tasks

entries.name: technical-design
entries.description: Structured approach to creating technical design documents from requirements

entries.name: test-analysis
entries.description: Analyze test results, identify failures, and determine root causes from test output

entries.name: analyze-image
entries.description: Describe, analyze, or extract information from images. Supports screenshots, diagrams, charts, photos, and UI mockups.

entries.name: codebase-explorer
entries.description: High-level codebase exploration for understanding project structure, patterns, and architecture. Combines directory listing, symbol search, and pattern recognition.

entries.name: docker-mcp
entries.description: Docker container management via MCP. List, start, stop, inspect containers, view logs, and manage images.

entries.name: fetch-mcp
entries.description: Make HTTP requests to fetch web content and APIs. Supports GET/POST with automatic content extraction and markdown conversion.

entries.name: file-search
entries.description: Fuzzy file path search. Find files when you know part of the name but not the exact location in the project tree.

entries.name: filesystem-mcp
entries.description: Sandboxed filesystem access via MCP. Read, write, search, and manage files within allowed directories.

entries.name: firecrawl-mcp
entries.description: Web scraping and crawling via MCP. Crawl websites, scrape pages, extract structured data, and convert content to markdown.

entries.name: get-diagnostics
entries.description: Check code files for compile errors, lint violations, type errors, and semantic issues. Preferred over running build commands for validation.

entries.name: git-history
entries.description: View recent git commits, diffs, and change history. Understand what changed, when, and by whom.

entries.name: github-mcp
entries.description: GitHub integration via MCP. Manage repositories, issues, pull requests, Actions workflows, branches, and file contents.

entries.name: grep-search
entries.description: Fast regex-based text search across files using ripgrep. Returns matches with file paths, line numbers, and context.

entries.name: list-directory
entries.description: List directory contents with optional recursive depth. Use to understand project structure before diving into specific files.

entries.name: memory-mcp
entries.description: Persistent knowledge graph for storing entities, relationships, and observations across sessions. Use for long-term memory and context retention.

entries.name: notion-mcp
entries.description: Notion workspace integration via MCP. Search, read, and create pages, manage databases, and query structured data.

entries.name: playwright-mcp
entries.description: Browser automation via Playwright MCP. Navigate pages, click elements, fill forms, take screenshots, and extract content from web pages.

entries.name: postgres-mcp
entries.description: PostgreSQL database access via MCP. Inspect schemas, run read-only queries, and explore database structure.

entries.name: read-code
entries.description: Read and analyze source code files with AST awareness. Navigate definitions, search symbols, and understand file structure.

entries.name: run-tests
entries.description: Execute the project test suite and return pass/fail results with failure details.

entries.name: sentry-mcp
entries.description: Sentry error monitoring via MCP. Search issues, view stack traces, analyze error trends, and resolve incidents.

entries.name: sequential-thinking-mcp
entries.description: Structured step-by-step reasoning via MCP. Break down complex problems into sequential thought steps with revision and branching support.

entries.name: shell-exec
entries.description: Execute arbitrary shell commands in the workspace. Use for build steps, linting, formatting, or any CLI operation not covered by a dedicated capability.

entries.name: slack-mcp
entries.description: Slack workspace integration via MCP. Read channels, send messages, manage threads, and search conversation history.

entries.name: summarize
entries.description: Summarize text, documents, code, or conversations into concise overviews. Supports multiple summary styles and configurable detail levels.

entries.name: web-search
entries.description: Search the web for documentation, examples, API references, and technical information.

entries.name: write-file
entries.description: Create or modify files in the workspace. Supports creating new files, replacing content, inserting at line numbers, and appending.

entries.name: decisions
entries.description: Architecture decisions, trade-offs, and rationale — prevents re-debating settled questions

entries.name: lessons-learned
entries.description: What worked, what didn't, patterns to avoid — prevents repeating mistakes

entries.name: project-context
entries.description: Tech stack, repo structure, conventions, and project-specific facts

entries.name: session-notes
entries.description: Current session state, progress, blockers, and observations to promote later

entries.name: team-context
entries.description: Team members, roles, communication channels, and org-wide conventions

entries.name: user-preferences
entries.description: User's coding style, tool preferences, communication style, and workflow habits

entries.name: api-design
entries.description: RESTful API design best practices — resource naming, HTTP methods, status codes, pagination, filtering, error responses, versioning, and authentication patterns. Includes OpenAPI spec guidance.

entries.name: brainstorming
entries.description: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

entries.name: brand-guidelines
entries.description: Applies Anthropic's official brand colors and typography to any sort of artifact that may benefit from having Anthropic's look-and-feel. Use it when brand colors or style guidelines, visual formatting, or company design standards apply.

entries.name: code-review
entries.description: Systematic code review with priority ordering — correctness, security, performance, readability, maintainability, style. Structured feedback with actionable suggestions.

entries.name: context-engineering
entries.description: Treat LLM context as a finite resource — minimal system prompts, token-efficient tools, progressive disclosure, compaction strategies, structured note-taking, and sub-agent architectures.

entries.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
entries.description: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies

entries.name: doc-coauthoring
entries.description: Guide users through a structured workflow for co-authoring documentation. Use when user wants to write documentation, proposals, technical specs, decision docs, or similar structured content. This workflow helps users efficiently transfer context, refine content through iteration, and verify the doc works for readers. Trigger when user mentions writing docs, creating proposals, drafting specs, or similar documentation tasks.

entries.name: documentation
entries.description: Write effective technical documentation — README structure, API docs, architecture decision records, inline comments, and progressive disclosure. Explain why, not what.

entries.name: executing-plans
entries.description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints

entries.name: frontend-design
entries.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with bold aesthetic direction. Design thinking before coding. Anti-AI-slop aesthetics with intentional typography, color, spacing, and animation.

entries.name: git-workflow
entries.description: Git branching strategies, conventional commit messages, PR best practices, code review etiquette, merge strategies, and release tagging for team-based development.

entries.name: internal-comms
entries.description: A set of resources to help me write all kinds of internal communications, using the formats that my company likes to use. Claude should use this skill whenever asked to write some sort of internal communications (status reports, leadership updates, 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQs, incident reports, project updates, etc.).

entries.name: mcp-builder
entries.description: Build Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers — protocol overview, tool and resource definitions, transport types (stdio, SSE, streamable HTTP), error handling, and testing strategies.

entries.name: performance-optimization
entries.description: Measurement-driven performance optimization — profile before optimizing, identify common bottlenecks (N+1 queries, bundle size, memory leaks, re-renders), and track web vitals.

entries.name: receiving-code-review
entries.description: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation

entries.name: requesting-code-review
entries.description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements

entries.name: security-audit
entries.description: Systematic security review covering OWASP Top 10, input validation, auth/authz, secrets management, dependency scanning, injection attacks, and XSS/CSRF prevention.

entries.name: skill-creator
entries.description: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.

entries.name: systematic-debugging
entries.description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

entries.name: test-driven-development
entries.description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

entries.name: vercel-react-best-practices
entries.description: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.

entries.name: verification-before-completion
entries.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always

entries.name: web-design-guidelines
entries.description: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".

entries.name: webapp-testing
entries.description: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.

entries.name: writing-plans
entries.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

entries.name: writing-skills
entries.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

entries.name: backup-on-delete
entries.description: Log deleted file paths for recovery — ensures no accidental data loss goes unnoticed

entries.name: diagnostics-after-write
entries.description: Run type checking and diagnostics after any source file edit to catch errors early

entries.name: guard-tool-use
entries.description: Log all tool invocations for audit trail and safety review

entries.name: lint-on-save
entries.description: Run linter with auto-fix when source files are edited to maintain consistent code style

entries.name: memory-on-session-end
entries.description: Prompt the agent to persist decisions, lessons, and preferences to memory when a workflow completes

entries.name: notify-workflow-failure
entries.description: Alert the user immediately when a workflow fails so they can decide to retry or escalate

entries.name: security-scan-on-commit
entries.description: Run a security audit before any commit to catch vulnerable dependencies early

entries.name: test-on-change
entries.description: Run related tests when source files change to catch regressions immediately

entries.name: build-feature
entries.description: End-to-end feature development workflow
